Da su   Evropa Bolesnici    stvorili   Ameriku  i Kanadu   kao i Novi Zeland,   I  Australiju,   zlikovačkim zločinima    ubijali su  narode,  Nas  male  narode  porobe  i naši  rade za  njih  Zlikovce.  Ljepa  Srpkinjo  Budi si srečna  kaj si  Srpkinja,  Srbi su jedini zdravi narod  u Evropu.   Russija  Poštena,,  je  veliki  zalogaj  za  ove  Zlikovce,,,  Zlikovci  uvijek  traže   male narode  koji su slabo naoružani,  onda  oni  dolaze  da  čine zločine  i pljačkaju, i tako se   Zapad  i Amerika obogatio,  na krvlju  i  zločinima.    Ovi  zlikovci  samo  Laju  kao bijesni psi,  oni  imaju  SMRTNI strah  od  Rusije,   Kina  im  je kao kost u grlu,  nemogu da  gutaju  ono što su  opljačkali u Srbiju,, Iraq  ,, Libiju ,, Syriju,   to ih  mnogo boli ,,,  Rusija  i  Kina  su se  radom poštenim  razvijali   i  dostigli  vruhunac  u  razvoju  pošteno.  Ovi zlikovci  Evropa i Amerika   zločinima, pljačkama,  GENOCIDOM,   demokratija  Aušvic  Dr.Josef Mengele,   Josef Goebbels Propaganda 2021  Evropa Adolfa Hitlera,   Napoleon,,  Benito Muslolini   i  ostali  koji  se  uvlače  Njemcima duboko  u Šupak,   kao i mi  hrvati  generacijama   (( za dom  u NJEMAČKE ŠUPKOVE  spremni )) da se hranimo  mi  bolesno gladni hrvati,  Demokratia  naša EVROPA Aušvic 2021.
Pitanje:   Dali   Naša Bolesna EVROPA Divljaci  👍  smije  da napadne  Rusiju  i  u  Rusiju  da  kidnapuju  malu  Rusku djecu  Da toj djeci  vade  ORGANE i da  Evropa prodaje te ORGANE po  Svetu  da se  bogati Evropa,   b e z    da  pita  Evropa  svoje  saveznike  ALBANSKE TERRORISTE.   Kao to što 1999 su   Evropa  i  ALBANSKI TERRORISTI činili  na  Srpsko Kosovo, kidnapovali   Srpsku  Malu djecu   vadili  ORGANE  prodavali po Svetu,  Karla Dell Ponte  Veli  Evropa se  brzo  bogatila  sa  ALBANSKIM TERRORISTIMA,  SVE TERRORISTE  U  Evropa Uniju  u  KREMATORIJE  u   mathausen  ili KlagEnfurt.
Mi smo >SLO, HR, BiH< 💖💖💖AUSTRIJSKI KONJUŠARI 10.Prosinac 2020 Joso Mesić Osnovna Škola Gundulićeva Z-P 10,000 Zagreb. ČINJENICE: ne može da registruje onaj koji je PATOLOŠKI BOLESNIK...Mi balkanci hrvati i Slovemci smo kao TRULI MRTVACI imamo ljudskih vrednosti kao TRULI MRTVACI -- mi smo Lažovi ,, Lopovi ,, Prevaranti ,, Manipulatori ,, Ratni Zlikovci samo zbog Pljačke , kao i naši NJemci mi se Njemcima uvlačimo duboko u ŠUPAK i zato nemožemo da se izlečimo.  (( Herce,,govna  Šujica  Srbo Perković)).
Mi Balkanci smo puni Podlih--Podmuklosti , Zlobe i Pakost i--Treba uvest pokretne Krematorije za sve Službenike i Političare od 1900 god. do 2020 koji su i najmanju štetu počinili svojim balkanskim narodima,,, NAŠI POLITIČARI I CRNOKOŠULJAŠI BALKANA NA GRANICAMA I PUTEVIMA PLJAČKAJU SVOJE GRAĐANE CRNOKOŠULJAŠI-BALKANCI OD OPLJAČKANOG NOVCA NA CARINAMA I PUTEVIMA 2008---2009 pljačkaju Optužuju i kažnjavaju žrtve Žrtve Crnokošuljaša , moraju sve još da plate,,Sud i sve ostalo,, udruženi službenici i Crnokošuljši BALKANA KOLIKO MORAJU DA DAJU POLITIČARIMA--LEŠINARIMA DA POLITIČARI LEŠINARI HRANE SVOJU BOLESNU DJECU I DA IH PRIPREMAJU DA NASTAVE DA PLJAČKAJU SVOJ NAROD BALKANA. 
Joso Mesić Zagreb. ČINJENICE: TKO VJERUJE NAMA HRVATIMA, TAJ JE PATOLOŠKI NEIZLEČIVI BOLESNIK. Nemože da razumije Evropa BOLESNIK ILI-PATOLOŠKI BOLESNIK EVROPA-Lopova, Lažova,Prevaranata. 
Kod nas hrvata u Saboru R-H 95% su ljepa Srpska prezimena ,, V I D O V I Ć ,,, Srbo,,ljub ZEKANOVIĆ ,, GLASNOVIĆ ,, ljepo sve plemeniti Srbin do Srbina --- neznamo da govorimo i pišemo hrvatski ZAGORSKI ,,,KAJKAVSKI ,, istrijaski... Pa mi bolesne budale hrvati lažovi ,, prevaranti ,, manipulatori ,, lopovi ,, ratni zlikovci sve samo zbog pljačke.
Volimo Srpski da Govorimo ,, čitamo pišemo ,, pjevamo Srpski ,, smejemo se Srpski. Mi hrvati za dom Spremni 2019 u NJemačke ŠUPkove da se hranimo. Josip Perkov.
Mi hrvati i slovenci smo mala iskompleksirana nacija 98% patološki bolesnici Lopovi , Prevaranti, ubice male djece 1991 Neznamo da pričamo hrvatski ,, pa volimo samo Srpski ,, jer smo budale ,, Mi hrvati se masovno iz hrvatske iseljavamo 1995 -- sada 2019 se masovno ( Za dom Spremni ) u NJemačke ŠUPKOVE useljavamo da se hranimo ,, kao to što su činili naši Djedovi i Pradjedovi ,,, jer nema više opljačkane hrane od naših građana Srba iz hrvatske ,, za nas bolesno gladne hrvate...
Ljepo li je hrvat Herce,,govna biti sa ljepim SRPSKIM PREZIMENOM u NJemački Šupak se uvlačiti. 

Vatikane Pederu prestanite da silujete malu djecu po Svetu ,,,, Ivane Sveti SRBINE Pavle ll drugi svaki hrvat u ŠUPAK te ljubi da se lečimo mi bolesni hrvati ,,, prijatno i dobar tek moji hrvati u NJemečki ŠUPAK, Dr. Sranjo Tuđman.
12.Studeni 2020..Ako ste kojim slučajem zdravi u patološki bolesnoj Evropi ,pogledat ćete na ZDF Info History: film 14 Stoleće CHRISTOPHER COLUMBO... FILM VATIKAN i USTAŠE 1941 KROATIEN... tko su najveći Sveta lažovi , i prevaranti ratni zlikovci samo zbog pljačke generacijama 1. Evropa 2.Amerika ovog Sveta ,, Evropa Adolfa Hitlera Politika hiljadama godina u našoj Evropi kod političara i službenika se nikad nije promenila na bolje za narode Sveta.1999 KOSOVO AUŠVIC EVROPA-Josef Mengele Aušvic DEMOKRATIA. NAŠA VOLJENA EVROPA DIVLJACI GENERACIJAMA ,, Kao naš HDZ--HRVATSKA DIVLJAKA ZAJEDNICA.
LJEPO LI JE HRVAT herce,,govna BITI GENERACIJAMA U NJEMAČKI ŠUPAK SE UVLAČITI DA SE HRANIMO MI HRVATI I BUDALE BOSANCI, A,,lija Izmet,,begović.
D E M O C I D JE IZRAZ KOJEG JE SKOVAO naš H R V A T  Politolog RUDO j, RUMEL S NAMJEROM OZNAČAVANJA ,, ŠIREG PODRUČJA ,, MASOVNIH - UBISTAVA POD ZAPOVEDNIŠTVOM VLASTI naše HRVATSKE ZBOG PLJAČKE,,,,ŠTO OZNAČAVA POJAM GENOCID ... D E M O C I D JE PREMA RUMMELU ,, SVAKA VRSTA DJELOVANJA OD STRANE naše VLASTI HRVATSKE ,, KOJA IMA PRVI CILJ IZRAVNO UBIJANJE male djece , SRBA,,JEVREJA,, I UZROKOVANJE UBIJANJE LJUDI ILI UZROKOVANJE SMRTI KOD LJUDI ,, NEMARA ILI PLJAČKE ,, i ODUZIMA ŽIVOT ŽRTVAMA KAO KOD NAS U HRVATSKU ŠTO SU ČINILI NAŠI HRVATI. D E M O C I D JE NAJVEĆI UZROK NEPRIRODNE S M R T I , KAO ŠTO SU TO ČINILI NAŠI slovenci 1991 i  mi   HRVATI. DALEKO ISPRED NESREĆA , RATOVA , KRIMINALA , ŽIVOTINJSKIH NAPADA ,, TO SMO MI HRVATI logor L O R A -- Genocidni SPLIT 1991 . DEMOCID je hiljadu puta gori od GENOCIDA ... Dr. Sranjo Tuđman i A,,lija Izmet,,begović.

1914.  EVROPA  AUŠVIC  KOSOVO. Bolesne  budale  Balkanci  neizlečivo bolesni ludaci, imamo  mnogo jeftino  kupljene  diplome i neizlečivo  bolesne  mozgove   Balkanci  mi  hrvati  i slovenci  ubice  male  djece. P I T A N J E :::Dali   naša  Evropa   Bolesnici   i  NATO-vci   smiju  da  napadnu   Crnu--Goru   ako se  Crna--Gora     izdvoji  iz  NATO  zlikovačke  organizacije,   bez  da  traže  pomoć  od  svojih  saveznika   ALBANSKIH  TERRORISTA,    i   dali  smiju   Evropa i NATO    da  zarate  sa  Rusijom,  a  da  ne traže  pomoć  od  svojih KOMADANATA  ALBANSKIH  TERRORISTA. Evropa  naša  Bolesnici.

comments.author: @dongraham4760
comments.text:
Larry C. Johnson– Within the first 24 hours of the Russian military operation in Ukraine, all Ukrainian Ground Radar Intercept capabilities were wiped out. Without those radars, the Ukrainian Air Force lost its ability to do air to air intercept. In the intervening three weeks, Russia has established a de facto No Fly Zone over Ukraine. While still vulnerable to shoulder fired Surface to Air Missiles supplied by the U.S. and NATO to the Ukrainians, there is no evidence that Russia has had to curtail Combat Air Operations.

Russia’s arrival in Kiev within three days of the invasion also caught my attention. I recalled that the Nazi’s in Operation Barbarossa took seven weeks to reach Kiev and the required 7 more weeks to subdue the city. The Nazis had the advantage of not pulling punches to avoid civilian casualties and were eager to destroy critical infrastructure. Yet many so-called American military experts claimed that Russia was bogged down. When a 24 mile (or 40 mile, depends on the news source) was positioned north of Kiev for more than a week, it was clear that Ukraine’s ability to launch significant military operations had been eliminated. If their artillery was intact, then that column was easy pickings for massive destruction. That did not happen. Alternatively, if the Ukrainian’s had a viable fixed wing or rotary wing capability they should have destroyed that column from the air. That did not happen. Or, if they had a viable cruise missile capability they should have rained down hell on the supposedly stalled Russian column. That did not happen. The Ukrainians did not even mount a significant infantry ambush of the column with their newly supplied U.S. Javelins.

The scale and scope of the Russian attack is remarkable. They captured territory in three weeks that is larger than the land mass of the United Kingdom. They then proceeded to carry out targeted attacks on key cities and military installations. We have not seen a single instance of a Ukrainian regiment or brigade size unit attacking and defeating a comparable Russian unit. Instead, the Russians have split the Ukrainian Army into fragments and cut their lines of communication. The Russians are consolidating their control of Mariupol and have secured all approaches on the Black Sea. Ukraine is now cut off in the South and the North.

I would note that the U.S. had a tougher time capturing this much territory in Iraq in 2003 while fighting against a far inferior, less capable military force. If anything, this Russian operation should scare the hell out of U.S. military and political leaders.

The really big news came this week with the Russian missile strikes on what are de facto NATO bases in Yavoriv and Zhytomyr. NATO conducted cyber security training at Zhytomyr in September 2018 and described Ukraine as a “NATO partner.” Zhytomyr was destroyed with hypersonic missiles on Saturday. Yavoriv suffered a similar fate last Sunday. It was the primary training and logistics center that NATO and EUCOM used to supply fighters and weapons to Ukraine. A large number of the military and civilian personnel at that base became casualties.

Not only is Russia striking and destroying bases used by NATO regularly since 2015, but there was no air raid warning and there was no shutdown of the attacking missiles.

comments.author: @Pepsicolandia
comments.text:
The true definition of madness,” Einstein reportedly said, “is repeating the same action, over and over, hoping for a different result.” Unfortunately, many proposals for ending the war on Ukraine ask the Ukrainians to repeat the same actions they have tried over and over with disastrous results. Those advocating for trying these approaches yet again bear a heavy burden of explaining why this time would be different. 



Many outcomes that may sound plausible to those uninformed about Putin’s history quite rightly look disastrous to Ukrainians. For example, Putin has said he wants a neutral, “demilitarized” Ukraine. Russia had that beginning in 1994, when Ukraine surrendered the nuclear weapons it inherited from the Soviet Union in exchange for guarantees of its existing boundaries from Russia, the United States, and the United Kingdom. 



Rather than allow this neutral, demilitarized Ukraine to live in peace within the longstanding boundaries Russia pledged to guarantee, Putin exploited Ukraine’s weakness to intervene in its politics and fix a presidential election for his deeply corrupt crony. When the Ukrainian people overthrew Putin’s puppet, Putin again took advantage of Ukraine’s weakness by seizing Crimea and a large part of Ukraine’s industrial heartland in the East. 



At some point, outsiders may tell the Ukrainians that they should accept a ceasefire at any price, even if it leaves Russian forces in their country. Ukraine did this after Russian’s 2008 invasion, with the promise of peace talks. 



Russia responded by stalling, shelling unoccupied parts of Ukraine, setting up two corrupt puppet regimes in its occupied territories — one of which shot down a Malaysian civilian airplane — and ultimately disavowing its agreement, to invade yet again. 



Nor are these isolated intrusions. Throughout the region, Russia has repeatedly seized parts of its neighbors’ territory, agreed to a ceasefire, and then continued its occupation without serious negotiations. It has occupied two regions of Georgia and one in Moldova for decades. Ukrainians know these “frozen conflicts” mean an indefinite loss of sovereignty, the indefinite subjugation of Ukrainians to Russian misrule, and a constant source of instability draining the country’s human and financial resources.

comments.text:
For those unaware, during WWII several American B-29 Superfortress bombers on missions over Japan were damaged by Japanese fighters and flak, necessitating the bombers not returning to their base in the Pacific islands but landing in the USSR which was closer.
The Soviet Union was at that time neutral against Japan so the American crews were detained and aircraft seized.
The B-29 was the most advanced aircraft in the world at that time.
Josef Stalin demanded that the aircraft be dismantled and duplicated EXACTLY.
Which is exactly what Soviet aeronautical technicians did.
Areas on the aircraft that had previously been damaged in combat and repaired (patches) were duplicated *precisely* .
It wasn't necessary to be that exact since the repairs were not part of the original finished product, but literally they dared not follow orders to the letter.

Or else.

So considering the Russian mindset, yeah this story is *totally legit* .

Ukraine under Kravchuk welcomed the idea of NATO enlargement. As president, he never opposed the expansion of the Alliance or the possibility of a future Ukrainian membership to NATO. This was reflected in his disdain for military cooperation with Eurasian structures, such as the Tashkent CIS Collective Security Treaty, in favour of European security structures. He said that "the best guarantee to Ukraine's security would be membership to NATO." He repeated his support for an immediate Ukrainian membership to NATO in 1994.

comments.author: @gragor11
comments.text:
" ... Drood said 2 days ago  @kosar kosar  
"the Ukrainians sent their army to the east and said they would take Crimea. "  You mean Crimea, which Russia took by force from Ukraine in 2014? How dare they suggest expelling foreign occupation. ... "



​ @Drood  pull your head out of your ass for a minute and look at the situation. 

Russia pushed the Tartars and Ottomans out of Crimea in 1783 to establish a naval base to project force in the Black and Mediterranean Seas.  

They paid an extremely heavy price defending their naval base in WW2 during the German sieges of Sevastopol.  
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Siege_of_Sevastopol_(1941%E2%80%931942)  Just in case you read.



In 1954, Crimea was handed to Ukraine SSR as a gift by Soviet leader Nikita Khrushchev, who was himself half-Ukrainian, which didn't really make much difference as it was still a Russian base under Russian control.  



Then we have the break up of the USSR with Ukraine and the Russians jointly controlling the Russian naval base at Sevastopol .....  



There is a theme here @Drood .  It has been a Russian base since they took it from the Ottomans.



So why does anyone in the west think that a US backed coup government in upstart Neo-Nazi Ukraine was ever going to get to keep one of the few non ice bound southern harbours that the Russian military has had control over for the last 238 years without a fight?  As that Wikipedia link highlighted, the Russians have paid a very high blood price indeed to maintain control of that base and access to it. As a Russian Ukrainian, it pains me very much to hear about the "annexation" of Crimea. Yes, from the point of view of international law, this act was illegal, but why does no one want to ask the residents of Crimea themselves? Why do Russians talk about Russian Crimea (which is more correct, but still not an argument), Ukrainians and the West talk about Ukrainian Crimea, but no one talks about Crimean Crimea? I know that most of the Crimeans live well in Russia, and that people were not forced to vote at gunpoint. Yes, not everyone voted for the return, just like not all Russians supported it, but the majority was in favor, and we must come to terms with this. Like, if the Crimeans wanted to join Zimbabwe, then this is their decision, lol. To resolve the conflict, I would propose a repeated referendum with the participation of international observers, but the Ukrainian oligarchs in power will never agree to this, because then the Crimea will be lost for them, and they will no longer be able to deceive and rob their own citizens under the guise of a "return" Crimea.
That regarding the war in Donbass, Putin clearly supports it, and I am very sorry that this is happening, but I don't think that the Russians started the war and that there really are regular Russian troops there. Not all Donbass supports a return to Russia, and we don't care if it stays in Ukraine or not, the war must end. However, this again will not work because of the interested oligarchs on both sides. I am ashamed of Russia, but we have bandits in power, and I sincerely urge you not to recognize it as a legitimate government, say that "Putin is Russia" and consider that Russia is bad. Russian people are really, truly great, but they have always had bad luck with rulers. comments.text:
Part 3. Let's go back in 1922 to the Treaty on the Creation of the USSR. In December 1991, the Supreme Council of Ukraine appealed to the parliaments and peoples of the world with a statement that now Ukraine considers the treaty on the formation of the USSR of 1922 not only invalid, but also not legitimate. That is, the USSR is a thing of the past and no laws and treaties of the USSR are recognized by Ukraine anymore. With all the disputes, shouts and emotions, the Crimean region was legally transferred to the Ukrainian Soviet Socialist Republic as part of the USSR, but not to the newly independent Ukraine (!!!!!!!!). That's the whole trick. That is, the leadership of Ukraine does not consider all the laws of the USSR to be valid, but at the same time they consider the agreement on the transfer of Crimea in 1954 to be valid (and indeed the rest of the territory of Ukraine, although these are Russian lands). Well, you get the idea, right? Nonsense. Where is the elementary logic here? And if we take into account the total decommunization in Ukraine, then 5 regions (annexed by the communists of the USSR) like it, not like it, but it is necessary to return Russia. With such a state of affairs, the inhabitants of Crimea strongly disagreed. Therefore, the will of the multinational people of Crimea to be a full-fledged subject of the union state of the USSR and a part of the Russian Federation was consistently expressed in three referendums: January 20, 1991, March 17, 1991 and December 1, 1991. And of course, March 16, 2014, which radically changed the fate of Crimea. If you look at what methods Ukraine regains its "Own" territories in the Donbass, then the Crimea escaped monstrous bloodshed thanks to Russia.
... As you know, in 2014 a coup took place in Ukraine. The legitimate president fled. Nazis - nationalists came to Power. Some regions of Ukraine and Crimea did not like this fact either. After that, the Presidium of the Autonomous Parliament of Crimea (which was elected long before these events and worked for many years in Crimea under Ukraine) stated that in Ukraine there had been "an unconstitutional seizure of power by radical nationalists with the support of armed gangs," and in connection with this situation, the regional parliament of Crimea "Takes full responsibility for the fate of Crimea", and "the only possible way out of this situation" is to apply the principles of direct democracy - calling and holding a referendum. In other words, Crimea did not recognize the legitimacy of the new Nazi government in Kiev. Kiev decided to hold Crimea by force. To prevent a bloody scenario, Russia, together with the Crimean self-defense units, blocked the Ukrainian armed forces in Crimea and provided the very possibility of a calm referendum. As for the term "Occupation": Russia did not send its troops to Crimea. Russian troops have always been there by agreement with Ukraine. I repeat - 26,000 Russian soldiers have ALWAYS been in Crimea under an agreement with Ukraine. Constantly. So look at Wikipedia for the definition of Occupation. There are two concepts of the country's integrity and the right of people to self-determination. In this case, there was the second option, since a referendum is the highest manifestation of democracy .. The population of Crimea chose self-determination. The Russian leadership, justifying aid to Crimea, refers to the UN Charter and the 1970 Declaration on the principles of international law that enshrine the right of peoples to self-determination, including "free accession or association with an independent state", which, according to the Russian Federation, was implemented "in extreme conditions and the impossibility of realizing [the population of Crimea] the right to self-determination within Ukraine, aggravated by the coming to power of illegal authorities, which in fact do not represent the entire Ukrainian people. " Just as an argument, Russia cites as an example a legal precedent - the Unilateral Recognition and Declaration of Independence of Kosovo. Russia has warned Europe and the United States many times not to do this. Otherwise, a dangerous legal precedent will arise. No need to open Pandora's Box. - warned Russia. However, no one listened to Russia. Now Russia has used this Legal precedent as an argument in its favor. At the same time, everyone suddenly began to mention international law. Russia allegedly violated it in relation to Crimea. I do not want to go into details, but I just want to remind you of Kosovo, which the collective West fully supported and recognized, and the international court ruled on the independence of Kosovo. So Kosovo gained independence only by the decision of the parliament, without a referendum, which is illegal and violates international law. Russia warned about this - it is not worth creating such a dangerous precedent in international practice, it is fraught with repetition. However, the West did not listen. So now we can talk and argue about international law endlessly - a precedent has emerged. And in Crimea, there was also a referendum in addition - whether someone likes it or not (this is a concrete fact).This is another event showing the effect of the Double Standards in international politics. There are thousands of such examples of Double Standards. What is permissible for some countries is not permissible for others (immediately sanctions, bullying in the press, military invasion, and so on). But the train, as they say, has already left. Since this is legal with Kosovo, now it is legal with the Crimea. Russia warned. Everything is clear here. So, Russia conquered Crimea from the Ottoman Empire (1783 !!!). Since then, Crimea has always been Russian. How Crimea became Ukrainian, we discussed at the very beginning. In other words, Ukraine has never legally owned Crimea since 1954. When this territory was called the Ukrainian Soviet Socialist Republic and had its own constitution, there were no problems.
 And once again to consolidate the lesson: Russia did not send troops into the Crimea. They were always there. Russian soldiers created a calm atmosphere during the referendum of 2014. A coup d'etat took place in Kiev. Nationalists, admirers of Hitler came to power. The inhabitants of Crimea did not want this new Nazi government and decided to hold a referendum on the future fate of Crimea. The Russian military, together with the local self-defense forces of Crimea (local residents), did not allow the nationalists from Kiev to Crimea. The referendum was organized and held by the long-elected legal authority of the Autonomous Republic of Crimea (NOT RUSSIAN). People voted for Russia (93%). The right of people to self-determination is enshrined in the UN Charter. Russia also followed in accordance with the legal precedent for the division of Kosovo.

comments.author: @giggling_boatswain
comments.text:
About why Crimea always remained Russian. (Legal aspect)
Part 1. Well, delve into the story. In general, the Russo-Turkish wars span a period of 351 years (1568-1918). During this period, Russia and Turkey were at war for 69 years. And in 1783 the Crimea was conquered by the Russians. In 1921, Crimea became autonomy (the Crimean Autonomous Republic) as part of Russia with the right to leave Russia, as an independent entity. This is indicated in the constitution of 1918. This means that even before the formation of the USSR, Crimea received statehood in the form of an autonomous republic within the Russian Federation. That is, in 1922, Crimea is an integral part of the Russian Federation. In the same 1922, the Autonomous Republic of Crimea became the founder of the creation of the USSR. That is, the autonomy of Crimea as part of the Russian Federation voted in favor of the creation of the USSR in 1922.
At that time, the Russian Federation included many republics, autonomous republics including Crimea. In particular, the representative of Crimea in the agreement on the creation of the USSR put his signature Said - Galiev (chairman of the Council of People's Commissars of the Crimean Autonomous Republic). That is, his signature is next to the signatures of Kalinin, Stalin and other persons. That is, Said-Galiev is signed as a representative of the Russian Federation, which includes Crimea. It is very important! That is, since the Autonomy of Crimea was a co-founder of the creation of the USSR, then, when the USSR collapsed, the opinion of the autonomous republic of Crimea should be taken into account. Further. After the formation of the USSR, for all citizens of the autonomous republic of Crimea (who already had citizenship of the Russian Federation), a single union citizenship of the USSR was established. That is, having citizenship of the USSR, residents of any republic also had the citizenship of this republic. This is enshrined in the agreement of December 30, 1922 on the creation of the USSR. What does this mean? This means that the Autonomous Republic of Crimea could leave the USSR, but at the same time remain a part of the Russian Federation. That is, when leaving the USSR to the Crimea, the laws of the USSR would cease to apply, but the laws of the Russian Federation would continue to apply, since the autonomous republic of Crimea was part of the Russian Federation. Now consider the following point: neither the 1921 treaty on the creation of the Autonomous Republic of Crimea, nor the USSR constitution, nor the constitution of the Russian Federation, nor the Crimea constitution provides for the possibility of abolishing the statehood of the autonomous republic. That is, nowhere in these four documents is there a word about the procedure for the liquidation of autonomy, in other words, it is impossible to deprive the Crimean Republic of autonomy. But on June 30, 1945, by an order of the Supreme Soviet of the USSR, the state autonomy of Crimea was illegally abolished, the Republic of Crimea was transformed into a region within the Russian Federation. Why did this happen? This decision was made due to the fact that a significant part of the Crimean population (as well as the Chechen population) actively collaborated with the Nazi troops. These peoples were totally evicted from their places of residence and completely displaced to Central Asia, which is certainly illegal. But Stalin solved the issues of betrayal in his own style. Thus, the southern part of the Chechen-Ingush Autonomous Soviet Socialist Republic was transferred to the Georgian Soviet Socialist Republic, and other regions of Chechen-Ingushetia became part of the Dagestan Autonomous Soviet Socialist Republic. Crimean autonomy has become the region of the Russian Federation (downgrade by many steps down). That is, the rights and opportunities of the Crimean region were much lower than that of the republics. Then, in 1954, by decree of the Presidium of the Supreme Soviet of the USSR, the Crimean region was transferred from the Russian Federation to the Ukrainian Soviet Socialist Republic (not to be confused with Ukraine - these are different things). But after the transfer of Crimea from the Russian Federation to the Ukrainian Soviet Socialist Republic, all Crimean residents continued to remain citizens of the Russian Federation because the issue of citizenship was not considered or raised during this transfer. Therefore, no documents on this issue were then created and do not exist. It was like moving a handkerchief from one pocket to another. Let me remind you that inside the USSR, along with a single common Union citizenship of the USSR, as in the Russian doll, there were separate citizenships of the Union republics and autonomies, each had its own. That is, the residents of Crimea never renounced their Russian citizenship. Even after the transfer of Crimea to the Ukrainian Soviet Socialist Republic. That is, de jure they have always been citizens of the Russian Federation, and since the inhabitants of Crimea never renounced the citizenship of the Russian Federation, when transferring the Crimean region from the Russian Federation to the Ukrainian Soviet Socialist Republic in 1954, citizenship was not changed from Russian to Ukrainian. I repeat - the inhabitants of Crimea constantly legally remained citizens of the Russian Federation.

comments.text:
About why Crimea always remained Russian. (Legal aspect)
Part 1. Well, delve into the story. In general, the Russo-Turkish wars span a period of 351 years (1568-1918). During this period, Russia and Turkey were at war for 69 years. And in 1783 the Crimea was conquered by the Russians. In 1921, Crimea became autonomy (the Crimean Autonomous Republic) as part of Russia with the right to leave Russia, as an independent entity. This is indicated in the constitution of 1918. This means that even before the formation of the USSR, Crimea received statehood in the form of an autonomous republic within the Russian Federation. That is, in 1922, Crimea is an integral part of the Russian Federation. In the same 1922, the Autonomous Republic of Crimea became the founder of the creation of the USSR. That is, the autonomy of Crimea as part of the Russian Federation voted in favor of the creation of the USSR in 1922.
At that time, the Russian Federation included many republics, autonomous republics including Crimea. In particular, the representative of Crimea in the agreement on the creation of the USSR put his signature Said - Galiev (chairman of the Council of People's Commissars of the Crimean Autonomous Republic). That is, his signature is next to the signatures of Kalinin, Stalin and other persons. That is, Said-Galiev is signed as a representative of the Russian Federation, which includes Crimea. It is very important! That is, since the Autonomy of Crimea was a co-founder of the creation of the USSR, then, when the USSR collapsed, the opinion of the autonomous republic of Crimea should be taken into account. Further. After the formation of the USSR, for all citizens of the autonomous republic of Crimea (who already had citizenship of the Russian Federation), a single union citizenship of the USSR was established. That is, having citizenship of the USSR, residents of any republic also had the citizenship of this republic. This is enshrined in the agreement of December 30, 1922 on the creation of the USSR. What does this mean? This means that the Autonomous Republic of Crimea could leave the USSR, but at the same time remain a part of the Russian Federation. That is, when leaving the USSR to the Crimea, the laws of the USSR would cease to apply, but the laws of the Russian Federation would continue to apply, since the autonomous republic of Crimea was part of the Russian Federation. Now consider the following point: neither the 1921 treaty on the creation of the Autonomous Republic of Crimea, nor the USSR constitution, nor the constitution of the Russian Federation, nor the Crimea constitution provides for the possibility of abolishing the statehood of the autonomous republic. That is, nowhere in these four documents is there a word about the procedure for the liquidation of autonomy, in other words, it is impossible to deprive the Crimean Republic of autonomy. But on June 30, 1945, by an order of the Supreme Soviet of the USSR, the state autonomy of Crimea was illegally abolished, the Republic of Crimea was transformed into a region within the Russian Federation. Why did this happen? This decision was made due to the fact that a significant part of the Crimean population (as well as the Chechen population) actively collaborated with the Nazi troops. These peoples were totally evicted from their places of residence and completely displaced to Central Asia, which is certainly illegal. But Stalin solved the issues of betrayal in his own style. Thus, the southern part of the Chechen-Ingush Autonomous Soviet Socialist Republic was transferred to the Georgian Soviet Socialist Republic, and other regions of Chechen-Ingushetia became part of the Dagestan Autonomous Soviet Socialist Republic. Crimean autonomy has become the region of the Russian Federation (downgrade by many steps down). That is, the rights and opportunities of the Crimean region were much lower than that of the republics. Then, in 1954, by decree of the Presidium of the Supreme Soviet of the USSR, the Crimean region was transferred from the Russian Federation to the Ukrainian Soviet Socialist Republic (not to be confused with Ukraine - these are different things). But after the transfer of Crimea from the Russian Federation to the Ukrainian Soviet Socialist Republic, all Crimean residents continued to remain citizens of the Russian Federation because the issue of citizenship was not considered or raised during this transfer. Therefore, no documents on this issue were then created and do not exist. It was like moving a handkerchief from one pocket to another. Let me remind you that inside the USSR, along with a single common Union citizenship of the USSR, as in the Russian doll, there were separate citizenships of the Union republics and autonomies, each had its own. That is, the residents of Crimea never renounced their Russian citizenship. Even after the transfer of Crimea to the Ukrainian Soviet Socialist Republic. That is, de jure they have always been citizens of the Russian Federation, and since the inhabitants of Crimea never renounced the citizenship of the Russian Federation, when transferring the Crimean region from the Russian Federation to the Ukrainian Soviet Socialist Republic in 1954, citizenship was not changed from Russian to Ukrainian. I repeat - the inhabitants of Crimea constantly legally remained citizens of the Russian Federation.

I'm from Donetsk/Donbass. It started in march of 2014, when Russian decided to implement the plan to invade another part of Ukraine(in february they invaded Crimea).

I remember clearly, I was walking on a street called "50 years of USSR" an there were over 50 busses with russian plates. There were only young men who should play a role as if Ukrainians and they hate Ukraine and they want to ask for help Russia.

comments.text:
This repeats itself again and again. Humanity actually never learnt from the tragedies of World War 2 and 1.
There is and always will be one Hitler or Putin, George Bush to start the carnage . All of them have one concern - they don't trust each other and therefore want to be prepared for a perceived trouble (sometimes an illusion). 
Putin's ultimate or only goal is to preserve the current map of the Russian Federation which of course contains lots of occupied land (from eastern territories such as Sakhalin which once belonged to Japan to western territories Caucasus and more). What Russia still has is a smaller version of USSR where the Central Asian states (although being independent) have no option but to bow to Russian Czar and seem to offer no trouble. Crimea was annexed recently but, Putler (new name for Putin) wants to ensure that every state bordering to Russia should be free from American, European influence and follow non-democratic system. He still mourns the loss of Georgia. 
Putin wants to protect Russia's massive land and he wants to retain this "smaller USSR" by hook or crook. 
If you ask me if ordinary Russians like him with all of his such patriotic visions for Russia, the answer is NO. Alexei Navalny exposed him and his system of elite corruption through his documentaries on YouTube. Things aren't the same again. 
Maybe 5 years before people had sort of mixed to positive feelings about him but, now they hate him. They see him as utterly corrupt and shameless liar who is there to protect his mafia state. I  believe 99% of the Russians deeply feel sorry for Navalny and believe Putin disgraced himself by everything he tried to do with Navalny - from that poisoning to cheap and fake accusations and trials.
There is no healthy  and unhealthy dictatorship - it's all bad and time has proven it. More a guy clings onto power, glues his back to the chair more his system, his surroundings get corrupt. Mr. Putin's pro Slavic, neonazi followers  also don't look at him the same way they did just few years ago. Alexei Navalny exposed him.

comments.author: @arinallen
comments.text:
I think the world would be better off if we disbanded our militaries and end military spending, cooperatively, world wide.  This can work if we all agree to it.  We might retain a small fraction of our military spending for a defensive national guard, and for some degree of coalition capacity necessitating international cooperation.  

I support self determination by the people affected in determining a course.  I support democracy.  Purely representative democracy, at it's best, is not good enough.  

I do believe that it is necessary to work in support of our allies and to aid our allies when necessary.  If Russia does invade the Ukraine I think the international community should commit itself, in cooperation with the Ukrainian government, by securing the airspace within Ukraine's borders.

I'd say put up EU and NATO drones now to patrol Ukraine's border, but what would we do if Putin shot some down?  They say it is easier to attack than to defend, so if an attack does come, let's make sure it is costly and unsuccessful.  If an attack does not come, then it winds up being expensive sabre rattling, and nothing more.  For now I think we should leave the burden with Russia, by doing nothing.

Hopefully, no invasion will occur.  

I think the Ukraine is clearly the peaceful neighbor here.  There are disputes within the Ukraine.  I hope these can be resolved democratically.

The US froze $640 BILLION in US denomination held by the Russian Central Bank. Russian government's annual budget is only $500 billion. That would be the equivalent of another country freezing $8.7 TRILLION belonging to the United States. That is a declaration of war.
Putin is *right now* the weakest and most vulnerable he has ever been, hiding in a bunker in the Urals. He does not have military support, he does not have the intelligence apparatus support, he doesn't have the support of his mafia lackeys (oligarchs), nor of the Russian people.

Putin has a cyanide capsule in his mouth as we speak.

comments.text: The trouble humanity has, and has always had, is one of self-definition. Before ‘human being’, first and foremost, we seem to want to put this or that random accident of birth, or this or that imagined daddy in the sky. Individually, this is not an issue. However, when we band together with others on the basis of random accidents of birth and imagined daddies in the sky, the trouble starts. Nietzsche looked at this in the eye and called it: *In individuals, insanity is rare; but in groups, parties, nations and epochs, it is the rule.* With an outlier like Putin, we need to develop a workable system of removing such dictators from positions of power. In the kindergarten, the staff don’t allow a single bully to ride roughshod over others as whim dictates. Why, then, do we allow this as adults? 👀

 8 "Out of anger, he is almost torn to pieces. Every time a crowd is thrown at him, the guard asks:" Did not a prophet (warner) come to you?

 10 And they say, If we had heard and understood, we would not have been among the dwellers of the Fire.

 11 So they confess their sins;  the dwellers of the Fire are far from mercy.

 12 Those who fear their Lord without seeing it, for them is forgiveness and a great reward.

 13 But hide yourselves, or speak your word openly;  surely He knows what you conceal in your hearts.

 14 Do not He Who created create, when He knows that He penetrates the depths of the secrets, He knows the details?

 15 He it is Who has made the earth suitable for you, so walk through its parts and enjoy His bounty, for to Him is the future.

 16 Have you been assured by Him Who is in the heavens that the earth may not shake you when it shakes?

 17 Were you assured of Him Who is in heaven, that He should not send upon you a tempest of stones?  Well, you will understand what My punishment is!

 18 Even those before them denied, but what was My punishment?

 19 Have they not seen the birds above him with open arms, and when the wings are folded, no one holds them in the air except the Almighty.  He is the One who sees and knows every thing.

 20 Who is your army that can help you but the Merciful?  It is true that the disbelievers are found in a great deception.

 21 Who is he that feedeth you, if He should withhold His provision from you?  Nay, but they persist in arrogance and aversion.

 22 Is he who walks with his face to the ground more guided, or he who walks upright on the straight path?

 23 Say: He it is Who created you, and endowed you with hearing and sight and understanding, but you give thanks a little.

 24 Say: "It is He Who created you and multiplied you in the earth, and to Him you will be gathered."

 25 And they say: When will this promise be fulfilled, if ye are truthful?

 26 Say: "Only Allah knows, and I am only a warner to you."

 27 "When they see it (Hell) approaching, the faces of those who disbelieve will be disfigured, and it will be said to them, 'This is what you used to seek.'

 28 Say: Tell me, if Allah takes my soul and those with me, or has mercy on us, who will protect the disbelievers from a painful doom?

 29 Say: He is the Most Merciful, to whom We have put our trust, and in Him we put our trust, and you will know who the lost one is.

 30 Say: Tell me, if your water is lost in the depths, who will bring you above ground water?

comments.text: There's a striking resemblance between Ukraine's fight against invading Russia and the Revolutionary War of the USA against England. In both cases Goliath didn't/isn't doing very well against the much smaller David. The greatest similarity is the difference in attitude. In the eighteenth century British soldiers had nothing against the fledgling American nation. Their hearts weren't in it. The US should have lost to the UK, but they were far more motivated than their British counterparts. We see the same thing happening now between Russia and Ukraine. Russia was supposed to roll over Ukraine relatively easily. But now Ukraine is actually taking the fight into Russia. The UK, as the much larger, established, better equipped military machine was defeated by the farmers-turned-soldiers of the smaller, ill-equipped, half-naked colonial upstarts. Is it possible that Ukraine can repeat this unlikely scenario? This war is bankrupting an already weak Russian economy. The average Russian citizen didn't want the war in the first place. Now battles are being fought in Russian towns and villages. Will Putin be forced to do a King George and give up, or will his iron-fisted pride result in the first usage of nuclear weapons since 1945?

comments.author: @jacobgoodstone7572
comments.text:
The real effects of this incursion won't be the land, strategic objectives, or resources taken by Ukraine, but the psychological impacts on both Russians and Ukrainians. For Russians, this is terrifying. If Putin can't defend Kursk how do they know he can defend their homes? For the Ukrainian soldiers, this is a massive morale boost after months of trench warfare and retreating. Not to mention, this incursion put Ukraine back in the headlines and proved that they aren't finished yet, which might spark a new wave of western aid to Ukraine. I don't know how Ukrainians managed to pull off this attack, but it might be the most crucial thing they've done in the war so far

Russia will inevitably hit the Ukrainians in Kursk really, really hard. This could result in a trench-warfare stalemate like what most of the frontline has evolved into, which tends to be extremely costly for Russia, so maybe Ukraine will manage to drain Russian forces while not actually giving up any of their own ground if they are forced to retreat. However, if Ukraine's supply lines can't handle the demands of the soldiers in this region, that could be disastrous. Also, if Russian manage to force the Ukrainians into a full-scale retreat we could see a sort of rout happening, where the Ukrainians are pushed out of Kursk and deep into their own territory, but I don't think that's likely

“The BBC's claim of Ukraine's incursion into Russia being the "biggest incursion since WW2" is likely an exaggeration and doesn't accurately reflect historical events[1]. Since World War II, there have been numerous military incursions between various countries, some of which were significantly larger in scale.

## Historical Context

Several notable incursions have occurred since World War II, including:

- The Korean War (1950-1953)
- The Vietnam War (1955-1975)
- The Soviet invasion of Afghanistan (1979-1989)
- The Iran-Iraq War (1980-1988)
- The Gulf War (1990-1991)

These conflicts involved large-scale military operations and territorial incursions that far surpass the recent events in Ukraine and Russia.”

- Perplexity

Foreign Domination: 120 years of lies and deception [Print Replica] Kindle Edition
by Thorsten Schulte (Author)  Format: Kindle Edition
==================================================================
This book is a warning call to all Europeans! Kurt Tucholsky wrote in 1931: "The people get most things wrong; but they feel most things right." More and more people feel that the future of Germany and Europe is in danger, but do not yet understand the causes. Therefore, the full extent of deception and foreign domination must be revealed. Only in this way can we recognize what is threatening us today, why it is evident that we are being governed against the will of the majority, and what we can do about it. In this book, Thorsten Schulte unmasks the historiography of the victors, exposes untruths, half-truths and the omission of important facts in our media. He exposes the distorted view of history that still leads to a guilt complex among Germans with devastating consequences. Only in this way does his analysis of the dangers in this book for today's Germany become comprehensible. We need to know our history - and the true history at that. The New York Times paid homage to the last German emperor in June 1913 with the now unthinkable headline: "Kaiser, 25 Years Ruler, Celebrated as Peacemaker". The Germans are demonstrably purposefully deprived of which U.S. representative met the later German dictator on November 20, 1922, and called him a "fabulous demagogue." Former U.S. President Harry S. Truman remarked about Germany and Russia in June 1941, "Let them exterminate each other as much as possible." Did German Chancellor Willy Brandt receive funds from the U.S. intelligence agency CIA? Were top Green Party politicians trained abroad? France's Le Figaro newspaper wrote in 1992 about the Maastricht Treaty, which paved the way for the Euro: "Maastricht, that's the Versailles Treaty without the war." Who could make Angela Merkel cry and with what in the euro bailout in 2011? Germany's and Europe's future is threatened if we do not act. We Europeans must use our eyes to see now, lest we use them to cry later. Courage to tell the truth for our children and grandchildren!

The Citadel Kindle Edition
by Robert Doherty (Author)  Format: Kindle Edition
=========================================
It was built to protect America. Now it could destroy the world. A pulse-pounding techno-thriller from the New York Times–bestselling author of Section 8.

At the start of the Cold War, the greatest threat to America wasn’t the Russians and the looming Communist threat. Rather, it was an elite organization bent on world domination, a group so powerful only nuclear weapons could safeguard against them. The CIA knew what these men were capable of, and in a last-ditch attempt to protect America against them, they built two high–security arsenals deep within the earth––one declassified in the Nevada desert, and one heavily under wraps in Antarctica. For over fifty years, no one spoke of The Citadel, the fortress deep under the ice in Antarctica that held the most powerful weapon known to man––until the Organization returned, hellbent on destruction.

Captain Jim Vaughn is a government agent known for performing missions no one else wants. So when an old colleague approaches him with an assignment, he can’t refuse––even if the mission has been set in motion by a dead man’s letter, found in Antarctica and dated 1949. The Citadel has been cracked, and the only man who can safeguard it is Vaughn. Nothing short of the fate of mankind rests on his shoulders.

Encounter: Close Encounter Of The Fifth Kind. (Frank Ryder Book 4) Kindle Edition
by Mike Woodhams (Author)  Format: Kindle Edition
==================================================================
RUN WITH MI6 OPERATIVE FRANK RYDER AS HE STRIVES TO FIND THE ABDUCTED SON OF THE BRITISH PRIME MINISTER AND FOIL THE RUSSIANS FROM DEPLOYING A WEAPON THAT WILL ALLOW THEM TO BECOME A DOMINATE WORLD POWER A strange signal is received by military listening stations in the Arctic. The source of the signal is determined to be from a Russian archipelago in the Barents Sea, but the signal cannot be fully deciphered and its location pinpointed.The British Prime Minister’s son, together with a celebrity naturalist and two camera crew, vanish whilst filming a nature documentary on the same archipelago. Could the Russians have abducted the four men? To find the source of the signal and what happened to the missing men, British Intelligence, send two MI6 operatives to the archipelago, accompanied by an elite international team of Special Forces. During the search, the special-forces team, led by Frank Ryder, ex SAS, stumble across a subterranean base, seemingly equivalent to America’s ‘Area 51’, where not only did they discover the missing men, but also a weapon the Russians are developing that will change the balance of world power. Ryder, his team, and the missing men, flee the base, but not before stealing evidence of the all-powerful Russian weapon soon to be unleashed. Guided by an Eskimo hunter, pursued by the Russians and encountering a series of hazardous escapades through rugged mountain terrain, Ryder makes a desperate bid to escape, despite an unexpected and shocking incident that seriously threatens the whole operation. Will Ryder escape the archipelago and return the Prime Minister’s son to safety? Will he be able to prevent the Russians from becoming a world power? ENCOUNTER is the fourth book in the Frank Ryder series.

The Second Coming of Christ: The Resurrection of the Christ Within You 2 Volume Set Paperback – Illustrated, Sep.1, 2008
by Paramahansa Yogananda (Author)
===============================
In this unprecedented masterwork of inspiration, Paramahansa Yogananda takes the reader on a profoundly enriching journey through the four Gospels. Verse by verse, he illumines the universal path to oneness with God taught by Jesus to his immediate disciples but obscured through centuries of misinterpretation: "how to become like Christ, how to resurrect the Eternal Christ within one's self".

This landmark work transcends divisive sectarianism to reveal a unifying harmony underlying all true religions. A groundbreaking synthesis of East and West, it imparts the life-transforming realization that each of us can experience for ourselves the promised Second Coming - awakening of the all-fulfilling Divine Consciousness latent within our souls.

Yogananda said, "In titling this work The Second Coming of Christ, I am not referring to a literal return of Jesus to earth. He came two thousand years ago and, after imparting a universal path to God's kingdom, was crucified and resurrected; his reappearance to the masses now is not necessary for the fulfillment of his teachings. What is necessary is for the cosmic wisdom and divine perception of Jesus to speak again through each one's own experience and understanding of the infinite Christ Consciousness that was incarnate in Jesus. That will be his true Second Coming."

comments.author: @denisvideography
comments.text:
The lie the West tells itself is that the war was “unprovoked.”

The word “unprovoked” is used repeatedly in President Biden’s major speech on the first anniversary of the war, in NATO statements, and in the media. The editorial pages of the New York Times alone include at least 26 editorials, opinion columns, and commentaries that describe the Russian invasion as “unprovoked.”

Yet the war and the Russian invasion were provoked by the issue of NATO expansion, just as leading American diplomats have been warning for decades.

There were actually two provocations related to NATO. The first was the US intention to expand NATO to Ukraine and Georgia, which would encircle Russia in the Black Sea region with NATO countries (Ukraine, Romania, Bulgaria, Turkey, and Georgia, in counterclockwise order).

The second was the US role in the violent overthrow of Ukraine’s pro-Russian president, Viktor Yanukovych, who had insisted on Ukraine’s neutrality. The shooting war began nine years ago with the installation of a US-backed, Russophobic government in Ukraine that is intent on joining NATO.

comments.text:
Regarding the topic of perfidy we see an interesting parallel in history developing before our sleepy eyes...
Of course, "history rhymes".
Of course the cogs of geopolitics turn slowly, and most people are far too pre-occupied to notice details...

It seems as if Russia/Putin is insisting on a written (words) and signed deal/accord with regard to the Ukraine.
*I wonder why?*

It seems as if he is threatening to station Russian troops in Cuba...again.
*I wonder why?*

Faced by "good guys", why could somebody possibly feel a need to insinst on a "contract" and a "signature", rather than just smily backpats, "pwomises" and  "shake hands"?

*Maybe it has something to do with past pwomises?*

Of course we can all google Foreign Secretary James "not an inch further East (NATO expansion)" Baker, to find out about the ton of promises made 30 years ago and which are now being "debated" and apologized as being "never were any promises made".

How history repeats itself.
Re. "preventive perfidy" (lol):
Agree something.
Put it down in words.
Sign.
Simple.
Certain people playing with *our* lives, never want to sign anything.

comments.author: @CitroChannel
comments.text:
Since I suspect that the US and most NATO countries will refuse to get involved in this conflict if Russia does invade Ukraine and it turns into a true hot war, I wonder how many individual fighters ("mercenaries") from other nations and/or PMCs will take advantage of the situation to fight for the Ukrankan side for money and/or "adventure" (and perhaps some just wanting to do what they think is the right thing?) even if it looks like a lost cause ultimately. I also wonder exactly how legal or illegal it would be for Brits, Americans, Canadians et al. that wanted to join the fight on the side of Ukraine without being part of a licensed PMC to do so. I know that there were unaffiliated individuals that did similar things against ISIL, but I don't know if they were breaking the law or not. Either way, I think it is very, very likely PMCs will find ways to get involved to make money if a serious conflict begins.  

Otherwise it is sad and unfortunate that Ukraine will be pretty much on its own against a country that it has no realistic hope against in a real war. At most I could maybe see Poland and/or some other eastern European countries that are also worried about Russia's increasingly hostile and expansionist desires offering support, but not in the form of actual manpower or troops and instead in terms of supplies, intelligence, funding, etc... Honestly I just feel bad for the people of Ukraine -- a country that largely wanted less corruption, more democracy, to join NATO, be more involved with central/western European powers and to join the EU will likely be left hanging when/if Russia decides to invade them because other powers aren't likely to want to get involved in a shooting war with Russia even if it means letting them illegally take over a sovereign nation and potentially kill a vast number of young soldiers defending their homeland as well as an unknown number of civilians if things really escalate.

comments.text:
I just want to remind my fellow humans out there that history may look very dark and many of us feel like it will continue to repeat itself over and over again, that we the people, always will be slaves to the ones on top who sets the rules.

Maybe I'm wrong about this but I think we're overlooking one major thing that's different in todays world, one HUGE thing actually, a relatively new weapon not of mass destruction but of mass resurrection, and that weapon is what makes it possible for me to reach out to you all who are reading this, it's called the Internet.

Now I know not everyone have access to this world wide means of communication, and that there's powers out there trying to control even this space, but the adoption of this technology keeps growing and even though it's still dark out there I can see the light that this brings in the form of people coming together in a way that was totally impossible before, the Internet has just been activated, it's not much older than 30 years, it's a newborn and its powers are still maturing, we have it, WE THE PEOPLE HAVE IT! Can't you see what this means? The possibilities we have here?

Remember that our police officers and military personnel also use the Internet, they read, and they see, they think and they feel, they are not ignorant, they are human like you, and like me, they are our fathers and mothers, our sisters and brothers, they are our children, they are us, we are them!

We need to start living a life guided by love and respect for each other, a life where we don't seek to harm each other but give it our best to understand our fellow human and make this planet a place for every one to feel at home on, and it all starts with me and you, yes you! You who reads this, it stats with us recognizing that deep down inside, we don't want to hurt any one, we want to feel useful and we want to be respected, let's try our best to give that respect to the ones we meet daily, to our neighbors, to our colleagues, give it to every one we meet, even if they don't give it back, and I promisse, we can actually turn this ship around and create a better reality for every one!

From the bottom of my heart and soul, I LOVE YOU and I RESPECT YOU!

(Please copy this message and spread it, spread it all over this place!)

comments.author: @Scorpio.1989
comments.text:
If NATO (or the EU or Both) cut off trade and flights to Russia, it would change their behavior real quick...

There are 3 NATO members in Russia's top 5 export trading partners

10.50% of Russia's trade is with Netherlands
6.57% of Russia's trade is with Germany
4.96% of Russia's trade is with Turkey

That alone is 22.03% of Russia's export trade.

On top of that Germany, The United States, and Italy are 3 of the top 5 largest suppliers of imports

10.16 of Russia's imports are from Germany
5.43% of Russia's imports are from The United States
4.41% of Russia's imports are from Italy

That's 20% of Russia's imports

Now, if all 30 NATO members cut off all trade with Russia,  would HURT Russia's economy far more than it'll hurt than each NATO member and be a serious blow to the Russian Ruble which is already next to useless...

Cutting off trade allows Russia to change their behavior, and return Crimea to Ukraine without a war, leaving war to being a absolute last resort (as it should be)...

And one of stipulations for reestablishing trade  should return the Crimea peninsula in it’s entirety along with all military equipment in the territory…

comments.author: @Raeat
comments.text:
I have questions. If Putin decides to use tactical nuclear weapons, they will almost certainly be used in the "annexed" territories. These territories are valuable for two main reasons: fossil fuels and wheat. If the nukes are used, these regions will become unusable for harvesting food or gaining natural resources. Russia is already the largest supplier of wheat in the world. Ukraine was the 5th largest. Putin wins in either event, nuclear or not. If the regions are uninhabitable, then mining will be out of the question and again Putin wins, since Russia is the largest exporter of fossil fuels as well. Ukraine loses access to these valuable resources on their lands and Russia remains the same. Meanwhile Ukraine's army gets obliterated in the process and Ukraine becomes destitute, it's primary resources gone.

If Putin decides to use nuclear weaponry, Russia itself MUST be devastated - not just the contained, "annexed" regions. Russia itself MUST become the battlefield.

Am I missing something here?

comments.text:
The US and Germany are not giving Ukraine the weapons they need to defend both themselves and western democracy because they are too busy sitting on the sidelines wetting their knickers in fright. Putin's argument is with the US and NATO with Ukraine being merely the first step in that fight. The Ukrainians need tanks, infantry fighting vehicles and long-range artillery (ATACMS) to dislodge the Russian forces in the coming close fight if they are to free the territories occupied by those forces. Also, where are the drones promised months ago.

Western democracy and freedom are at stake here. The world needs to see leadership from the US at this time. If Ukraine loses or cedes territory to Russia Putin will feel confident enough to challenge NATO, but, if Ukraine wins then it is very unlikely that Putin will attempt any military action against NATO given that he can't defeat the Ukrainians.

If Putin attacks a NATO country then the US and the West will most definitely be involved in a major war.

comments.author: @A.Hunter279
comments.text:
It must be very embarrassing for the Russians to have their airspace violated like this by an enemy that uses such outdated equipment, but modified in a very clever way. Just a few days ago, Russian analysts proudly proclaimed that Ukraine's new F-16s will have no impact whatsoever and that the only role in which they could be used would be that of kamikaze drones. 

And here we have a Cessna 152 or 172, a plane with a cruise speed of roughly 100 knots flying deep inside Russia without being intercepted. I mean, these Cessnas are among the slowest and least sophisticated aircraft in the world. Almost every pilot must have flown one in the early stages of his or her career, but what is truly shocking is that Russia (a country which operates advanced aircraft like the Su-35 and Su-57 and powerful anti-aircraft missiles like the S 400 and the BUK) seems completely unable to shoot down a slow and tiny Cessna. 

This is beyond mind-boggling, because even in peacetime civilian aircraft are not allowed to enter an airspace without first establishing radio contact and while having their transponder turned off. This Cessna must have appeared in Russian radars as an unidentified traffic for several hours, but nothing was done to identify it or try to bring it down. Such negligence is inexcusable.

comments.author: @pr0xZen
comments.text:
Kreml is huge. Doing actual substantial damage would require an air bombing raid, or a large barrage of very heavy ballistic or crusise missiles, not drones. Much of Kreml is built to withstand traditional aerial bombardment too. But at the end of the day, Ukraine wouldn't really achieve anything favorable doing so even of they had the capacity, not strategically and certainly not politically. Most likely it would only galvanize support for Russia's war, and potentially even justify the use of nuclear weapons in accord with the self defense section of their nuclear doctrine.

Ukraine is far better served by targeting Russian logistic, military production & repair, and state revenue assets. Working to deteriorate Russia's _practical and financial ability_ to fight this war, rather than _Putin's political will_ to do so.

Secondary, Kreml is pretty heavily defended at this point. By striking high value assets in Russia far from Moscow, it pressures the Russians to redistribute their air defense assets, pretty far away from Ukraine.
